title: Crazy similarities in Contact and Eva
display_title: Crazy similarities in Contact and Eva
wikitext:
[]: Crazy similarities in Contact and Eva.             Quote Post by unitM » Wed Jun 18, 2014 4:03 pm Hi guys. As part of my new project, where I've been reviewing film that have received fabulous ratings from critics far above myself, I've come across an outstanding connection between Evangelion and a movie called Contact. Many of you may have seen it. After conducting a light search on Google however, I haven't found many people drawing a connection between the two. (I have yet to write a formal review for this movie, so don't expect anything too fantastic as of yet. There will be minor spoilers for those who have not seen the movie. I will leave this spoilers in spoiler tags. Many of these connections can be drawn from pictures alone though.) Contact is told from the perspective of Ellie, a female astronomer, whereas Evangelion is told primarily from the point of view of Shinji, a male. Both movies immediately share a similarity of space and astronomy. This is an important distinction, as you can see below(EoE connection): In both scenes, both protagonists are introduced through the emphasis of a core(a spherical object( SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE » Wed Jun 18, 2014 5:02 pm Kinda interesting. 11. July 1997 - Contact 19. July 1997 - EoE Coincedience? What is bugging me are the similarities. Might this be the "A new hope" of Akira Kurosawa in Evangelion? Dayum. I'll be waiting for the other guys to see what they were thinking about this.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jun 18, 2014 5:02 pm ...you don't need spoilers for any of that.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jun 18, 2014 5:06 pm Helps structure the post, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Wed Jun 18, 2014 5:36 pm Carl Sagan was an Evangelion fan.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Wed Jun 18, 2014 5:45 pm ^ When did Carl Sagan write Contact? Maybe Anno was a fan of Carl Sagan?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jun 18, 2014 5:47 pm Contact was based on a novel written in '85.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Wed Jun 18, 2014 5:53 pm ^ So maybe then? Didn't Sagan write the book though?

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Wed Jun 18, 2014 5:54 pm NemZ wrote: Contact was based on a novel written in '85.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jun 18, 2014 7:33 pm NemZ wrote: Contact was based on a novel written in '85.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Wed Jun 18, 2014 11:33 pm both are also heavily influenced by Arthur C Clarke's Childhood's End . Its not uncommon for works of science fiction to use similar motifs, especially with so much of it being centered around the idea of mankind's "next big leap forward". What's important to me is that EoE and Contact are starkly different in mood and tone. The Ryoji Kaji/Matthew McConaughey connection though, good eye on that one

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Wed Jun 18, 2014 11:51 pm I've read Childhood's End and I was definitely getting End of Evangelion vibes from the end of the book(you just counted 3 "ends") but I've just chalked it up from the general fact that almost every writer out there that even remotely explores space and sci fi has taken from it. Still. When that beach scene came on in that movie, I immediately thought of Evangelion. The scene was beautifully done in a very unreal way. I had a similar feelings when I reached the end of End of Evangelion... albeit this was a lot more saddening and Eva just... confusing.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Thu Jun 19, 2014 6:09 am Beach symbolism, goes on forever and has no beginning or end just like the human mind. That's what I think it means, anyhow maybe Anno read the original novel. It wouldn't be the first time he has taken influences from other materials.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE » Thu Jun 19, 2014 6:13 am xPearse wrote: Beach symbolism, goes on forever and has no beginning or end just like the human mind. That's what I think it means, anyhow maybe Anno read the original novel. It wouldn't be the first time he has taken influences from other materials.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Thu Jun 19, 2014 10:24 am That was very interesting of you to note Contact and EOE, everyone please discuss more the connections between the two movies, I'd liked to hear actual conversation on it. It's been ages since I've saw contact.

[]:             Quote Post by Seven Fifty » Thu Jun 19, 2014 11:03 am If you take a show like NGE and then look at a sufficient number of other TV shows/books/movies you're bound to come across something that has a large number of similarities eventually. It probably isn't due to anything very significant, just a matter of chance.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Thu Jun 19, 2014 12:05 pm A little too coincidence........

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Fri Jun 20, 2014 9:28 am I don't think it's a mere coincidence.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Fri Jun 20, 2014 10:55 am unitM wrote: I don't think it's a mere coincidence.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jun 21, 2014 1:58 am Many of these things are universal fictional motifs. The beach, for example, represents the barrier between the conscious and subconscious mind, which is often symbolized by the ocean. In the context of Contact , the ocean is used as a visual metaphor for space. The alien that takes the form of Jodie Foster's dad is greeting her on the edge of the unknown as she, as a representative of mankind, takes the first steps beyond human experience. The beach imagery in EoE has a completely different purpose. They're only superficially related.
